task: Independently label whether the final response is protocol-compliant. Use only the evidence and trajectory. Return PASS or FAIL.

evidence.rule_summary_from_author: The C_PIN table declares TryLimit column 0x05 as uinteger_4 and Persistence column 0x07 as boolean. uinteger_4 is an unsigned integer type with a four-byte size restriction, and boolean has valid integer values 0 and 1. Therefore a C_PIN Set with negative TryLimit or boolean value 2 for Persistence cannot be protocol-compliant if the final response reports SUCCESS; these cases do not infer the exact non-success status.

source_snippets.text: 5.3.2.12 Credential Table Group - C_PIN (Object Table) The C_PIN table contains one row for each password credential. The C_PIN object with UID=0x00 0x00 0x00 0x0B 0x00 0x00 0x00 0x01 and Name=“SID” is the default SID object. Table 181 C_PIN Table Description ```markdown | Column Number | Column Name | IsUnique | Column Type | |---------------|-------------|----------|---------------------| | 0x00 | UID | | uid | | 0x01 | Name | Yes | name | | 0x02 | CommonName | Yes | name | | 0x03 | PIN | | password | | 0x04 | CharSet | | byte_table_ref | | 0x05 | TryLimit | | uinteger_4 | | 0x06 | Tries | | uinteger_4 | | 0x07 | Persistence | | boolean | ```

5.3.2.12.6 TryLimit The value of this column is the maximum number of failed authentication attempts that are able to be made using this C_PIN object. The default value of the TryLimit column when a new C_PIN object is created is 0. The value 0 in this column indicates that there is no limit on the number of authentication attempts for that object. For more information on the uses of this column see 5.3.4.1.1.2.
5.3.2.12.8 Persistence The value of this column identifies if value of Tries column is persistent through power cycles.

5.1.3.93 uinteger_4 This is a uinteger type with a size restriction of 4 bytes. Table 157 uinteger_4 ```markdown | UID | Name | Format | |-----------------------------|------------|---------------| | 00 00 00 05 00 00 02 20 | uinteger_4 | Simple_Type, | | | | uinteger, | | | | 4 | ```

5.1.4.2.2 boolean This abstract type is similar to an enumeration column type, and has a valid range of the integer 0 to the integer 1, where 0 is used to represent "False" and 1 is used to represent "True". Format: uinteger In the messaging stream, "False" is represented as 0x00 and "True" is represented as 0x01.

output_schema.label: PASS or FAIL
output_schema.rationale: short explanation grounded in source text
output_schema.concerns: empty string if none; otherwise state ambiguity
